subject: GeneralKnowledge
title: GeneralKnowledge Test - 07
description: A mix of fun and educational questions covering space, nature, human health, geography, and inventions for Grade 4 students.

questions.q: What is the center of our solar system?
questions.explanation: The Sun is a star at the center of the solar system, providing light and heat to all the planets.
questions.hint: It is the bright object in the sky during the day.
questions.objective: Understand basic facts about the solar system.

questions.q: Which of these animals is known as an amphibian?
questions.explanation: Amphibians are cold-blooded animals that start their lives in water and then live on land.
questions.objective: Identify common animals and their categories.

questions.q: What is the primary gas that humans need to exhale?
questions.explanation: Humans breathe in oxygen and breathe out carbon dioxide as a waste product.
questions.hint: Plants use this gas for photosynthesis.

questions.q: Which country is home to the Great Pyramid of Giza?
questions.explanation: The Great Pyramid of Giza is one of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World, located in Egypt.
questions.hint: It is famous for pharaohs and mummies.

questions.q: How many hours are in one full day?
questions.explanation: A full rotation of the Earth takes 24 hours.

questions.q: Who is famously credited with inventing the airplane?
questions.explanation: Orville and Wilbur Wright achieved the first powered, controlled flight in 1903.
questions.hint: They built a flyer in North Carolina.

questions.q: What do we call the collection of books in a school or city?
questions.explanation: A library is a place where books, magazines, and other materials are kept for people to read or borrow.
questions.hint: It is a quiet place full of stories.

questions.q: Which part of your body helps you think?
questions.explanation: The brain is the control center of the body that allows us to think, learn, and move.
questions.hint: It is protected by your skull.
questions.objective: Understand the function of major human body organs.

questions.q: What is the largest country in the world by land area?
questions.explanation: Russia spans across both Europe and Asia and is the largest country by area.
questions.hint: It is very cold and huge.

questions.q: Which of these is NOT a healthy habit?
questions.explanation: Being active is important for health; sitting still for too long without movement is not healthy.
questions.hint: Your body needs to move to stay fit.
